briefing.headline: Rig up a Football Jig — finesse summer approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Football Jig is a strong choice for summer with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: When fish are stuck to the bottom in heavy current, snap a 3/4-oz football jig — not stroking it (for suspended fish) but a small snap, let it fall, snap, let it fall. This makes pressured, bottom-hugging fish react

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: When fish are stuck to the bottom in heavy current, snap a 3/4-oz football jig — not stroking it (for suspended fish) but a small snap, let it fall, snap, let it fall. This makes pressured, b
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, hump, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: A snapped football jig catches fish from pressured busted-up schools that won't bite other techniques — especially effective without FFS, just covering water with precise casts
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, hump, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: Snapping a football jig is different from stroking — stroking is for suspended fish, snapping is a small sharp motion that makes bottom-hugging fish in heavy current react. The higher-frequency kick action of the Cleanup Craw trailer comp...

Johnston: Nobody remembers who finishes second in the Classic, so throw big baits and go for the win. Expect big swimbaits, deep-diving crankbaits, Carolina rigs, and football jigs to be the best chances.

Palaniuk: 'When marking fish on electronics that won't bite big cranks or football jigs, drop a Neko rig — the first fish was a 9+. When I kept it in my hand I could go to every single brushpile and catch one'

Hackney: 'Quite a few fish live shallow even during the hottest time of year. They hold in shade of boat docks, overhanging trees, lily pads, and grass. Hot weather triggers grass growth that creates shade, oxygen, and ambush points.'

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal summer fish retreat to reliable offshore structure at 12-25ft. They're less aggressive but still need to eat. Focus on primary structure — main lake points, ledges, and humps with shade or current.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
